name in kana (P1814) == * やぶたじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7190005006026 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県伊賀市馬場951番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:陽夫多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24870457}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Yabuta Shrine |画像= [[File:陽夫多神社01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}951 Baba |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= Unknown
(currently {{ill||lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}; originally possibly {{ill||lt=Taga no Muraji|UNKNOWN}} (Takamatsu deity) and Yabuta deity) |社格= {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
Former {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}})? |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} ({{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}) |別名= Yabuta Shrine, Takamatsu Gion, Kawai Tennō, Yabuta Gion-sha, Takamatsu Gion-sha |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715}} - {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}} |神事= Kakko Odori ({{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}})
Negai-no-Yama Odori ({{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}})
Rarashi (Rara Festival) ({{ill|lt=February 18|Q2343|WD=Q2343}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Uga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yabuta Shrine''' (やぶたじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=inner-listed shrine||WD=|lt=listed shrine}} (small shrine) in Baba, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Yabuta Shrine is an ancient shrine counted both as a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and a {{ill|lt=Kokushi genzaisha|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|lt=Kokushi-genzai shrine}}, but its divine lineage is unclear. According to 'Tō Yabuta Jinja-ki,' while noting that the 'Engi Fudoki' is likely spurious, it records: "There was a deity on Kawaiyama in {{ill|lt=Abe District|Q11657437|WD=Q11657437}}, called Yabuta Myōjin; in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|lt=Takeohirokuni Oshitate no Sumeramikoto}}, year {{ill|lt=Tsuchinoe-Uma|Q905011|WD=Q905011}}, the {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673}} Taga no Muraji worshiped it; the deity worshiped was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}." Taga no Muraji were a lineage in {{ill|lt=Taga|Q1346747|WD=Q1346747}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, tracing ancestry to {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858}}; thus worship of Susanoo, child of Izanagi, is plausible. In '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}},' Yabuta deity is treated as the Shikinaisha, while Takamatsu deity is treated as a non-Shikinaisha. In other words, Yabuta deity and Takamatsu deity are considered different. Takamatsu deity already held the rank of Senior Sixth, then in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 3 (861) was promoted to Junior Fifth Lower together with Takakura deity, Awa deity, and Unane deity. In Jōgan 15, Awa deity and Unane deity were promoted again and entered the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, but Takakura deity and Yabuta deity did not advance and remained Kokushi genzaisha outside the Engishiki list. One interpretation is that the clan venerating Yabuta deity lost power in inter-clan political struggles. Takamatsu deity's lineage is also unclear, but {{ill|lt=Torigoe Kenzaburō|Q28354686|WD=Q28354686}} inferred a {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi-line}} connection, based on frequent use of the character "高" in Katsuragi-related divine names. From these studies, identifying Takamatsu deity and Yabuta deity as the same remains questionable. In a written petition by priests of Yabuta Shrine is the statement: "In ancient times, from Kawai village (present site), about five chō east, from a place called Fujiyama, the deity was transferred to this shrine." Since Fujiyama referred to the alluvial zone of the {{ill||lt=Tomoda River|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill||lt=Kawai River|UNKNOWN}}, Yabuta deity was likely an agricultural deity worshiped there. === Cultural properties === === Mie Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * Gion Festival Negai-no-Yama rite === Iga City designated cultural properties === * Bonshō (temple bell) * Otabisho Kofun === Festivals === Rites such as the Kinen festival and Gion festival are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/85 Special Shinto Rites in Mie Prefecture]. === Anecdotes === * In the precincts is a dry {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} called "Baba no Miyai." It is said water springs from it only on Yabuta Shrine's festival day. This parallels the "Wakasa-i" rite at {{ill||lt=Nigatsu-dō|UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} and is one legend born from sacred-water belief tied to agricultural pre-harvest rites. * At the {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} on the south side stands a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} called {{ill|lt=Otabisho Kofun (Iga)||WD=|lt=Otabisho Kofun}}, with its stone chamber exposed. The buried person is unknown and the tomb was looted. * Multiple kofun have also been confirmed on Miyayama behind the shrine, the grove revered as {{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}}. Together with Otabisho Kofun, they form the {{ill|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group (Iga)||WD=|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group}}. In the mountain are also medieval and Sengoku-period castle remains, and a walking route is maintained as "Yabuta Kofun no Mori." * In {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544}} 5 (1473), {{ill|lt=Kampaku|Q1199966|WD=Q1199966}} and {{ill|lt=Daijō-daijin|Q1157586|WD=Q1157586}} {{ill|lt=Ichijō Kaneyoshi|Q1656375|WD=Q1656375}} visited Yabuta Shrine and composed this poem while crossing Takamatsu Bridge (once in the precincts). {{Quotation|Unable to cross, swept by this floating world's waves; stepping on Kawai's bridge is perilous.|Ichijō Kaneyoshi|'Fuchigawa no Ki'}} :It is said to liken the dangerous bridge during the {{ill|lt=rainy season|Q658486|WD=Q658486|lt=samidare}} floods to the war-torn ruin of many provinces. === Sources === Source is 'Kosasato no Ayumi Ayama-chō' (Ayama Town Board of Education, ed.) (1980). The "Overview" section draws on pp.151-154; the "Anecdotes" section on pp.326 and 334. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related shrines === *{{ill||lt=Yabu ni Imasu Shrine|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyōgo)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}) *{{ill||lt=Yabuta Myōjin|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|WD=Q11409326}}) *{{ill||lt=Yafuta Shrine|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Kawakuri District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊賀市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀市の歴史]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * やぶたじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7190005006026 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県伊賀市馬場951番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:陽夫多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24870457}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Yabuta Shrine |画像= [[File:陽夫多神社01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}951 Baba |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= Unknown
(currently {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}; originally possibly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taga no Muraji|UNKNOWN}} (Takamatsu deity) and Yabuta deity) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}})? |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} ({{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}) |別名= Yabuta Shrine, Takamatsu Gion, Kawai Tennō, Yabuta Gion-sha, Takamatsu Gion-sha |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715}} - {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}} |神事= Kakko Odori ({{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}})
Negai-no-Yama Odori ({{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}})
Rarashi (Rara Festival) ({{ill|lt=February 18|Q2343|WD=Q2343}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Uga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yabuta Shrine''' (やぶたじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=inner-listed shrine||WD=|lt=listed shrine}} (small shrine) in Baba, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Yabuta Shrine is an ancient shrine counted both as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and a {{ill|lt=Kokushi genzaisha|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|lt=Kokushi-genzai shrine}}, but its divine lineage is unclear. According to 'Tō Yabuta Jinja-ki,' while noting that the 'Engi Fudoki' is likely spurious, it records: "There was a deity on Kawaiyama in {{ill|lt=Abe District|Q11657437|WD=Q11657437}}, called Yabuta Myōjin; in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|lt=Takeohirokuni Oshitate no Sumeramikoto}}, year {{ill|lt=Tsuchinoe-Uma|Q905011|WD=Q905011}}, the {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673}} Taga no Muraji worshiped it; the deity worshiped was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}." Taga no Muraji were a lineage in {{ill|lt=Taga|Q1346747|WD=Q1346747}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, tracing ancestry to {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858}}; thus worship of Susanoo, child of Izanagi, is plausible. In '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}},' Yabuta deity is treated as the Shikinaisha, while Takamatsu deity is treated as a non-Shikinaisha. In other words, Yabuta deity and Takamatsu deity are considered different. Takamatsu deity already held the rank of Senior Sixth, then in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 3 (861) was promoted to Junior Fifth Lower together with Takakura deity, Awa deity, and Unane deity. In Jōgan 15, Awa deity and Unane deity were promoted again and entered the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, but Takakura deity and Yabuta deity did not advance and remained Kokushi genzaisha outside the Engishiki list. One interpretation is that the clan venerating Yabuta deity lost power in inter-clan political struggles. Takamatsu deity's lineage is also unclear, but {{ill|lt=Torigoe Kenzaburō|Q28354686|WD=Q28354686}} inferred a {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi-line}} connection, based on frequent use of the character "高" in Katsuragi-related divine names. From these studies, identifying Takamatsu deity and Yabuta deity as the same remains questionable. In a written petition by priests of Yabuta Shrine is the statement: "In ancient times, from Kawai village (present site), about five chō east, from a place called Fujiyama, the deity was transferred to this shrine." Since Fujiyama referred to the alluvial zone of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomoda River|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawai River|UNKNOWN}}, Yabuta deity was likely an agricultural deity worshiped there. === Cultural properties === === Mie Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * Gion Festival Negai-no-Yama rite === Iga City designated cultural properties === * Bonshō (temple bell) * Otabisho Kofun === Festivals === Rites such as the Kinen festival and Gion festival are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/85 Special Shinto Rites in Mie Prefecture]. === Anecdotes === * In the precincts is a dry {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} called "Baba no Miyai." It is said water springs from it only on Yabuta Shrine's festival day. This parallels the "Wakasa-i" rite at {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigatsu-dō|UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} and is one legend born from sacred-water belief tied to agricultural pre-harvest rites. * At the {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} on the south side stands a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} called {{ill|lt=Otabisho Kofun (Iga)||WD=|lt=Otabisho Kofun}}, with its stone chamber exposed. The buried person is unknown and the tomb was looted. * Multiple kofun have also been confirmed on Miyayama behind the shrine, the grove revered as {{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}}. Together with Otabisho Kofun, they form the {{ill|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group (Iga)||WD=|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group}}. In the mountain are also medieval and Sengoku-period castle remains, and a walking route is maintained as "Yabuta Kofun no Mori." * In {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544}} 5 (1473), {{ill|lt=Kampaku|Q1199966|WD=Q1199966}} and {{ill|lt=Daijō-daijin|Q1157586|WD=Q1157586}} {{ill|lt=Ichijō Kaneyoshi|Q1656375|WD=Q1656375}} visited Yabuta Shrine and composed this poem while crossing Takamatsu Bridge (once in the precincts). {{Quotation|Unable to cross, swept by this floating world's waves; stepping on Kawai's bridge is perilous.|Ichijō Kaneyoshi|'Fuchigawa no Ki'}} :It is said to liken the dangerous bridge during the {{ill|lt=rainy season|Q658486|WD=Q658486|lt=samidare}} floods to the war-torn ruin of many provinces. === Sources === Source is 'Kosasato no Ayumi Ayama-chō' (Ayama Town Board of Education, ed.) (1980). The "Overview" section draws on pp.151-154; the "Anecdotes" section on pp.326 and 334. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related shrines === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabu ni Imasu Shrine|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyōgo)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabuta Myōjin|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|WD=Q11409326}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yafuta Shrine|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Kawakuri District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊賀市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀市の歴史]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * やぶたじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7190005006026 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県伊賀市馬場951番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:陽夫多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24870457}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Yabuta Shrine |画像= [[File:陽夫多神社01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}951 Baba |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= Unknown
(currently {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; originally possibly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taga no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (Takamatsu deity) and Yabuta deity) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}})? |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} ({{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}) |別名= Yabuta Shrine, Takamatsu Gion, Kawai Tennō, Yabuta Gion-sha, Takamatsu Gion-sha |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715}} - {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}} |神事= Kakko Odori ({{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}})
Negai-no-Yama Odori ({{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}})
Rarashi (Rara Festival) ({{ill|lt=February 18|Q2343|WD=Q2343}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Uga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yabuta Shrine''' (やぶたじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=inner-listed shrine||WD=|lt=listed shrine}} (small shrine) in Baba, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Yabuta Shrine is an ancient shrine counted both as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and a {{ill|lt=Kokushi genzaisha|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|lt=Kokushi-genzai shrine}}, but its divine lineage is unclear. According to 'Tō Yabuta Jinja-ki,' while noting that the 'Engi Fudoki' is likely spurious, it records: "There was a deity on Kawaiyama in {{ill|lt=Abe District|Q11657437|WD=Q11657437}}, called Yabuta Myōjin; in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|lt=Takeohirokuni Oshitate no Sumeramikoto}}, year {{ill|lt=Tsuchinoe-Uma|Q905011|WD=Q905011}}, the {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673}} Taga no Muraji worshiped it; the deity worshiped was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}." Taga no Muraji were a lineage in {{ill|lt=Taga|Q1346747|WD=Q1346747}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, tracing ancestry to {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858}}; thus worship of Susanoo, child of Izanagi, is plausible. In '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}},' Yabuta deity is treated as the Shikinaisha, while Takamatsu deity is treated as a non-Shikinaisha. In other words, Yabuta deity and Takamatsu deity are considered different. Takamatsu deity already held the rank of Senior Sixth, then in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 3 (861) was promoted to Junior Fifth Lower together with Takakura deity, Awa deity, and Unane deity. In Jōgan 15, Awa deity and Unane deity were promoted again and entered the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, but Takakura deity and Yabuta deity did not advance and remained Kokushi genzaisha outside the Engishiki list. One interpretation is that the clan venerating Yabuta deity lost power in inter-clan political struggles. Takamatsu deity's lineage is also unclear, but {{ill|lt=Torigoe Kenzaburō|Q28354686|WD=Q28354686}} inferred a {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi-line}} connection, based on frequent use of the character "高" in Katsuragi-related divine names. From these studies, identifying Takamatsu deity and Yabuta deity as the same remains questionable. In a written petition by priests of Yabuta Shrine is the statement: "In ancient times, from Kawai village (present site), about five chō east, from a place called Fujiyama, the deity was transferred to this shrine." Since Fujiyama referred to the alluvial zone of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomoda River|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawai River|1=UNKNOWN}}, Yabuta deity was likely an agricultural deity worshiped there. === Cultural properties === === Mie Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * Gion Festival Negai-no-Yama rite === Iga City designated cultural properties === * Bonshō (temple bell) * Otabisho Kofun === Festivals === Rites such as the Kinen festival and Gion festival are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/85 Special Shinto Rites in Mie Prefecture]. === Anecdotes === * In the precincts is a dry {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} called "Baba no Miyai." It is said water springs from it only on Yabuta Shrine's festival day. This parallels the "Wakasa-i" rite at {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigatsu-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} and is one legend born from sacred-water belief tied to agricultural pre-harvest rites. * At the {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} on the south side stands a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} called {{ill|lt=Otabisho Kofun (Iga)||WD=|lt=Otabisho Kofun}}, with its stone chamber exposed. The buried person is unknown and the tomb was looted. * Multiple kofun have also been confirmed on Miyayama behind the shrine, the grove revered as {{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}}. Together with Otabisho Kofun, they form the {{ill|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group (Iga)||WD=|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group}}. In the mountain are also medieval and Sengoku-period castle remains, and a walking route is maintained as "Yabuta Kofun no Mori." * In {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544}} 5 (1473), {{ill|lt=Kampaku|Q1199966|WD=Q1199966}} and {{ill|lt=Daijō-daijin|Q1157586|WD=Q1157586}} {{ill|lt=Ichijō Kaneyoshi|Q1656375|WD=Q1656375}} visited Yabuta Shrine and composed this poem while crossing Takamatsu Bridge (once in the precincts). {{Quotation|Unable to cross, swept by this floating world's waves; stepping on Kawai's bridge is perilous.|Ichijō Kaneyoshi|'Fuchigawa no Ki'}} :It is said to liken the dangerous bridge during the {{ill|lt=rainy season|Q658486|WD=Q658486|lt=samidare}} floods to the war-torn ruin of many provinces. === Sources === Source is 'Kosasato no Ayumi Ayama-chō' (Ayama Town Board of Education, ed.) (1980). The "Overview" section draws on pp.151-154; the "Anecdotes" section on pp.326 and 334. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related shrines === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabu ni Imasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyōgo)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabuta Myōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|WD=Q11409326}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yafuta Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Kawakuri District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊賀市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀市の歴史]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * やぶたじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7190005006026 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県伊賀市馬場951番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:陽夫多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24870457}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Yabuta Shrine |画像= [[File:陽夫多神社01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}951 Baba |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= Unknown
(currently {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; originally possibly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taga no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (Takamatsu deity) and Yabuta deity) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}})? |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} ({{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}) |別名= Yabuta Shrine, Takamatsu Gion, Kawai Tennō, Yabuta Gion-sha, Takamatsu Gion-sha |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715}} - {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}} |神事= Kakko Odori ({{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}})
Negai-no-Yama Odori ({{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}})
Rarashi (Rara Festival) ({{ill|lt=February 18|Q2343|WD=Q2343}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Uga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yabuta Shrine''' (やぶたじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=inner-listed shrine|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=listed shrine}} (small shrine) in Baba, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Yabuta Shrine is an ancient shrine counted both as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and a {{ill|lt=Kokushi genzaisha|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|lt=Kokushi-genzai shrine}}, but its divine lineage is unclear. According to 'Tō Yabuta Jinja-ki,' while noting that the 'Engi Fudoki' is likely spurious, it records: "There was a deity on Kawaiyama in {{ill|lt=Abe District|Q11657437|WD=Q11657437}}, called Yabuta Myōjin; in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|lt=Takeohirokuni Oshitate no Sumeramikoto}}, year {{ill|lt=Tsuchinoe-Uma|Q905011|WD=Q905011}}, the {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673}} Taga no Muraji worshiped it; the deity worshiped was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}." Taga no Muraji were a lineage in {{ill|lt=Taga|Q1346747|WD=Q1346747}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, tracing ancestry to {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858}}; thus worship of Susanoo, child of Izanagi, is plausible. In '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}},' Yabuta deity is treated as the Shikinaisha, while Takamatsu deity is treated as a non-Shikinaisha. In other words, Yabuta deity and Takamatsu deity are considered different. Takamatsu deity already held the rank of Senior Sixth, then in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 3 (861) was promoted to Junior Fifth Lower together with Takakura deity, Awa deity, and Unane deity. In Jōgan 15, Awa deity and Unane deity were promoted again and entered the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, but Takakura deity and Yabuta deity did not advance and remained Kokushi genzaisha outside the Engishiki list. One interpretation is that the clan venerating Yabuta deity lost power in inter-clan political struggles. Takamatsu deity's lineage is also unclear, but {{ill|lt=Torigoe Kenzaburō|Q28354686|WD=Q28354686}} inferred a {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi-line}} connection, based on frequent use of the character "高" in Katsuragi-related divine names. From these studies, identifying Takamatsu deity and Yabuta deity as the same remains questionable. In a written petition by priests of Yabuta Shrine is the statement: "In ancient times, from Kawai village (present site), about five chō east, from a place called Fujiyama, the deity was transferred to this shrine." Since Fujiyama referred to the alluvial zone of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomoda River|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawai River|1=UNKNOWN}}, Yabuta deity was likely an agricultural deity worshiped there. === Cultural properties === === Mie Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * Gion Festival Negai-no-Yama rite === Iga City designated cultural properties === * Bonshō (temple bell) * Otabisho Kofun === Festivals === Rites such as the Kinen festival and Gion festival are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/85 Special Shinto Rites in Mie Prefecture]. === Anecdotes === * In the precincts is a dry {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} called "Baba no Miyai." It is said water springs from it only on Yabuta Shrine's festival day. This parallels the "Wakasa-i" rite at {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigatsu-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} and is one legend born from sacred-water belief tied to agricultural pre-harvest rites. * At the {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} on the south side stands a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} called {{ill|lt=Otabisho Kofun (Iga)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Otabisho Kofun}}, with its stone chamber exposed. The buried person is unknown and the tomb was looted. * Multiple kofun have also been confirmed on Miyayama behind the shrine, the grove revered as {{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}}. Together with Otabisho Kofun, they form the {{ill|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group (Iga)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group}}. In the mountain are also medieval and Sengoku-period castle remains, and a walking route is maintained as "Yabuta Kofun no Mori." * In {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544}} 5 (1473), {{ill|lt=Kampaku|Q1199966|WD=Q1199966}} and {{ill|lt=Daijō-daijin|Q1157586|WD=Q1157586}} {{ill|lt=Ichijō Kaneyoshi|Q1656375|WD=Q1656375}} visited Yabuta Shrine and composed this poem while crossing Takamatsu Bridge (once in the precincts). {{Quotation|Unable to cross, swept by this floating world's waves; stepping on Kawai's bridge is perilous.|Ichijō Kaneyoshi|'Fuchigawa no Ki'}} :It is said to liken the dangerous bridge during the {{ill|lt=rainy season|Q658486|WD=Q658486|lt=samidare}} floods to the war-torn ruin of many provinces. === Sources === Source is 'Kosasato no Ayumi Ayama-chō' (Ayama Town Board of Education, ed.) (1980). The "Overview" section draws on pp.151-154; the "Anecdotes" section on pp.326 and 334. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related shrines === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabu ni Imasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyōgo)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabuta Myōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|WD=Q11409326}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yafuta Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Kawakuri District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊賀市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀市の歴史]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q24870457}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Yabuta Shrine |画像= [[File:陽夫多神社01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}951 Baba |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= Unknown
(currently {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}; originally possibly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taga no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (Takamatsu deity) and Yabuta deity) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}})? |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} ({{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}) |別名= Yabuta Shrine, Takamatsu Gion, Kawai Tennō, Yabuta Gion-sha, Takamatsu Gion-sha |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715}} - {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}} |神事= Kakko Odori ({{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}})
Negai-no-Yama Odori ({{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}})
Rarashi (Rara Festival) ({{ill|lt=February 18|Q2343|WD=Q2343}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Uga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yabuta Shrine''' (やぶたじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=inner-listed shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=listed shrine}} (small shrine) in Baba, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Yabuta Shrine is an ancient shrine counted both as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and a {{ill|lt=Kokushi genzaisha|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|lt=Kokushi-genzai shrine}}, but its divine lineage is unclear. According to 'Tō Yabuta Jinja-ki,' while noting that the 'Engi Fudoki' is likely spurious, it records: "There was a deity on Kawaiyama in {{ill|lt=Abe District|Q11657437|WD=Q11657437}}, called Yabuta Myōjin; in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|lt=Takeohirokuni Oshitate no Sumeramikoto}}, year {{ill|lt=Tsuchinoe-Uma|Q905011|WD=Q905011}}, the {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673}} Taga no Muraji worshiped it; the deity worshiped was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}." Taga no Muraji were a lineage in {{ill|lt=Taga|Q1346747|WD=Q1346747}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, tracing ancestry to {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858}}; thus worship of Susanoo, child of Izanagi, is plausible. In '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}},' Yabuta deity is treated as the Shikinaisha, while Takamatsu deity is treated as a non-Shikinaisha. In other words, Yabuta deity and Takamatsu deity are considered different. Takamatsu deity already held the rank of Senior Sixth, then in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 3 (861) was promoted to Junior Fifth Lower together with Takakura deity, Awa deity, and Unane deity. In Jōgan 15, Awa deity and Unane deity were promoted again and entered the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, but Takakura deity and Yabuta deity did not advance and remained Kokushi genzaisha outside the Engishiki list. One interpretation is that the clan venerating Yabuta deity lost power in inter-clan political struggles. Takamatsu deity's lineage is also unclear, but {{ill|lt=Torigoe Kenzaburō|Q28354686|WD=Q28354686}} inferred a {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi-line}} connection, based on frequent use of the character "高" in Katsuragi-related divine names. From these studies, identifying Takamatsu deity and Yabuta deity as the same remains questionable. In a written petition by priests of Yabuta Shrine is the statement: "In ancient times, from Kawai village (present site), about five chō east, from a place called Fujiyama, the deity was transferred to this shrine." Since Fujiyama referred to the alluvial zone of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomoda River|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawai River|1=UNKNOWN}}, Yabuta deity was likely an agricultural deity worshiped there. === Cultural properties === === Mie Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * Gion Festival Negai-no-Yama rite === Iga City designated cultural properties === * Bonshō (temple bell) * Otabisho Kofun === Festivals === Rites such as the Kinen festival and Gion festival are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/85 Special Shinto Rites in Mie Prefecture]. === Anecdotes === * In the precincts is a dry {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} called "Baba no Miyai." It is said water springs from it only on Yabuta Shrine's festival day. This parallels the "Wakasa-i" rite at {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigatsu-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} and is one legend born from sacred-water belief tied to agricultural pre-harvest rites. * At the {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} on the south side stands a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} called {{ill|lt=Otabisho Kofun (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Otabisho Kofun}}, with its stone chamber exposed. The buried person is unknown and the tomb was looted. * Multiple kofun have also been confirmed on Miyayama behind the shrine, the grove revered as {{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}}. Together with Otabisho Kofun, they form the {{ill|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group}}. In the mountain are also medieval and Sengoku-period castle remains, and a walking route is maintained as "Yabuta Kofun no Mori." * In {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544}} 5 (1473), {{ill|lt=Kampaku|Q1199966|WD=Q1199966}} and {{ill|lt=Daijō-daijin|Q1157586|WD=Q1157586}} {{ill|lt=Ichijō Kaneyoshi|Q1656375|WD=Q1656375}} visited Yabuta Shrine and composed this poem while crossing Takamatsu Bridge (once in the precincts). {{Quotation|Unable to cross, swept by this floating world's waves; stepping on Kawai's bridge is perilous.|Ichijō Kaneyoshi|'Fuchigawa no Ki'}} :It is said to liken the dangerous bridge during the {{ill|lt=rainy season|Q658486|WD=Q658486|lt=samidare}} floods to the war-torn ruin of many provinces. === Sources === Source is 'Kosasato no Ayumi Ayama-chō' (Ayama Town Board of Education, ed.) (1980). The "Overview" section draws on pp.151-154; the "Anecdotes" section on pp.326 and 334. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related shrines === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabu ni Imasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyōgo)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabuta Myōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|WD=Q11409326}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yafuta Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Kawakuri District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊賀市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀市の歴史]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * やぶたじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7190005006026 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県伊賀市馬場951番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:陽夫多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24870457}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Yabuta Shrine |画像= [[File:陽夫多神社01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}951 Baba |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= Unknown
(currently {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; originally possibly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taga no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (Takamatsu deity) and Yabuta deity) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}})? |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} ({{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}) |別名= Yabuta Shrine, Takamatsu Gion, Kawai Tennō, Yabuta Gion-sha, Takamatsu Gion-sha |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715}} - {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}} |神事= Kakko Odori ({{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}})
Negai-no-Yama Odori ({{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}})
Rarashi (Rara Festival) ({{ill|lt=February 18|Q2343|WD=Q2343}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Uga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yabuta Shrine''' (やぶたじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=inner-listed shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=listed shrine}} (small shrine) in Baba, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Yabuta Shrine is an ancient shrine counted both as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and a {{ill|lt=Kokushi genzaisha|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|lt=Kokushi-genzai shrine}}, but its divine lineage is unclear. According to 'Tō Yabuta Jinja-ki,' while noting that the 'Engi Fudoki' is likely spurious, it records: "There was a deity on Kawaiyama in {{ill|lt=Abe District|Q11657437|WD=Q11657437}}, called Yabuta Myōjin; in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|lt=Takeohirokuni Oshitate no Sumeramikoto}}, year {{ill|lt=Tsuchinoe-Uma|Q905011|WD=Q905011}}, the {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673}} Taga no Muraji worshiped it; the deity worshiped was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}." Taga no Muraji were a lineage in {{ill|lt=Taga|Q1346747|WD=Q1346747}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, tracing ancestry to {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858}}; thus worship of Susanoo, child of Izanagi, is plausible. In '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}},' Yabuta deity is treated as the Shikinaisha, while Takamatsu deity is treated as a non-Shikinaisha. In other words, Yabuta deity and Takamatsu deity are considered different. Takamatsu deity already held the rank of Senior Sixth, then in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 3 (861) was promoted to Junior Fifth Lower together with Takakura deity, Awa deity, and Unane deity. In Jōgan 15, Awa deity and Unane deity were promoted again and entered the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, but Takakura deity and Yabuta deity did not advance and remained Kokushi genzaisha outside the Engishiki list. One interpretation is that the clan venerating Yabuta deity lost power in inter-clan political struggles. Takamatsu deity's lineage is also unclear, but {{ill|lt=Torigoe Kenzaburō|Q28354686|WD=Q28354686}} inferred a {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi-line}} connection, based on frequent use of the character "高" in Katsuragi-related divine names. From these studies, identifying Takamatsu deity and Yabuta deity as the same remains questionable. In a written petition by priests of Yabuta Shrine is the statement: "In ancient times, from Kawai village (present site), about five chō east, from a place called Fujiyama, the deity was transferred to this shrine." Since Fujiyama referred to the alluvial zone of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomoda River|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawai River|1=UNKNOWN}}, Yabuta deity was likely an agricultural deity worshiped there. === Cultural properties === === Mie Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * Gion Festival Negai-no-Yama rite === Iga City designated cultural properties === * Bonshō (temple bell) * Otabisho Kofun === Festivals === Rites such as the Kinen festival and Gion festival are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/85 Special Shinto Rites in Mie Prefecture]. === Anecdotes === * In the precincts is a dry {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} called "Baba no Miyai." It is said water springs from it only on Yabuta Shrine's festival day. This parallels the "Wakasa-i" rite at {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigatsu-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} and is one legend born from sacred-water belief tied to agricultural pre-harvest rites. * At the {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} on the south side stands a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} called {{ill|lt=Otabisho Kofun (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Otabisho Kofun}}, with its stone chamber exposed. The buried person is unknown and the tomb was looted. * Multiple kofun have also been confirmed on Miyayama behind the shrine, the grove revered as {{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}}. Together with Otabisho Kofun, they form the {{ill|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group}}. In the mountain are also medieval and Sengoku-period castle remains, and a walking route is maintained as "Yabuta Kofun no Mori." * In {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544}} 5 (1473), {{ill|lt=Kampaku|Q1199966|WD=Q1199966}} and {{ill|lt=Daijō-daijin|Q1157586|WD=Q1157586}} {{ill|lt=Ichijō Kaneyoshi|Q1656375|WD=Q1656375}} visited Yabuta Shrine and composed this poem while crossing Takamatsu Bridge (once in the precincts). {{Quotation|Unable to cross, swept by this floating world's waves; stepping on Kawai's bridge is perilous.|Ichijō Kaneyoshi|'Fuchigawa no Ki'}} :It is said to liken the dangerous bridge during the {{ill|lt=rainy season|Q658486|WD=Q658486|lt=samidare}} floods to the war-torn ruin of many provinces. === Sources === Source is 'Kosasato no Ayumi Ayama-chō' (Ayama Town Board of Education, ed.) (1980). The "Overview" section draws on pp.151-154; the "Anecdotes" section on pp.326 and 334. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related shrines === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabu ni Imasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyōgo)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabuta Myōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|WD=Q11409326}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yafuta Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Kawakuri District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊賀市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀市の歴史]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q24870457}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Yabuta Shrine |画像= [[File:陽夫多神社01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}951 Baba |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= Unknown
(currently {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; originally possibly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taga no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (Takamatsu deity) and Yabuta deity) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}})? |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} ({{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}) |別名= Yabuta Shrine, Takamatsu Gion, Kawai Tennō, Yabuta Gion-sha, Takamatsu Gion-sha |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715}} - {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}} |神事= Kakko Odori ({{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}})
Negai-no-Yama Odori ({{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}})
Rarashi (Rara Festival) ({{ill|lt=February 18|Q2343|WD=Q2343}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Uga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yabuta Shrine''' (やぶたじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=inner-listed shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=listed shrine}} (small shrine) in Baba, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Yabuta Shrine is an ancient shrine counted both as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and a {{ill|lt=Kokushi genzaisha|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|lt=Kokushi-genzai shrine}}, but its divine lineage is unclear. According to 'Tō Yabuta Jinja-ki,' while noting that the 'Engi Fudoki' is likely spurious, it records: "There was a deity on Kawaiyama in {{ill|lt=Abe District|Q11657437|WD=Q11657437}}, called Yabuta Myōjin; in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|lt=Takeohirokuni Oshitate no Sumeramikoto}}, year {{ill|lt=Tsuchinoe-Uma|Q905011|WD=Q905011}}, the {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673}} Taga no Muraji worshiped it; the deity worshiped was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}." Taga no Muraji were a lineage in {{ill|lt=Taga|Q1346747|WD=Q1346747}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, tracing ancestry to {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858}}; thus worship of Susanoo, child of Izanagi, is plausible. In '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}},' Yabuta deity is treated as the Shikinaisha, while Takamatsu deity is treated as a non-Shikinaisha. In other words, Yabuta deity and Takamatsu deity are considered different. Takamatsu deity already held the rank of Senior Sixth, then in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 3 (861) was promoted to Junior Fifth Lower together with Takakura deity, Awa deity, and Unane deity. In Jōgan 15, Awa deity and Unane deity were promoted again and entered the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, but Takakura deity and Yabuta deity did not advance and remained Kokushi genzaisha outside the Engishiki list. One interpretation is that the clan venerating Yabuta deity lost power in inter-clan political struggles. Takamatsu deity's lineage is also unclear, but {{ill|lt=Torigoe Kenzaburō|Q28354686|WD=Q28354686}} inferred a {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi-line}} connection, based on frequent use of the character "高" in Katsuragi-related divine names. From these studies, identifying Takamatsu deity and Yabuta deity as the same remains questionable. In a written petition by priests of Yabuta Shrine is the statement: "In ancient times, from Kawai village (present site), about five chō east, from a place called Fujiyama, the deity was transferred to this shrine." Since Fujiyama referred to the alluvial zone of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomoda River|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawai River|1=UNKNOWN}}, Yabuta deity was likely an agricultural deity worshiped there. === Cultural properties === === Mie Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * Gion Festival Negai-no-Yama rite === Iga City designated cultural properties === * Bonshō (temple bell) * Otabisho Kofun === Festivals === Rites such as the Kinen festival and Gion festival are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/85 Special Shinto Rites in Mie Prefecture]. === Anecdotes === * In the precincts is a dry {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} called "Baba no Miyai." It is said water springs from it only on Yabuta Shrine's festival day. This parallels the "Wakasa-i" rite at {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigatsu-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} and is one legend born from sacred-water belief tied to agricultural pre-harvest rites. * At the {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} on the south side stands a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} called {{ill|lt=Otabisho Kofun (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Otabisho Kofun}}, with its stone chamber exposed. The buried person is unknown and the tomb was looted. * Multiple kofun have also been confirmed on Miyayama behind the shrine, the grove revered as {{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}}. Together with Otabisho Kofun, they form the {{ill|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group}}. In the mountain are also medieval and Sengoku-period castle remains, and a walking route is maintained as "Yabuta Kofun no Mori." * In {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544}} 5 (1473), {{ill|lt=Kampaku|Q1199966|WD=Q1199966}} and {{ill|lt=Daijō-daijin|Q1157586|WD=Q1157586}} {{ill|lt=Ichijō Kaneyoshi|Q1656375|WD=Q1656375}} visited Yabuta Shrine and composed this poem while crossing Takamatsu Bridge (once in the precincts). {{Quotation|Unable to cross, swept by this floating world's waves; stepping on Kawai's bridge is perilous.|Ichijō Kaneyoshi|'Fuchigawa no Ki'}} :It is said to liken the dangerous bridge during the {{ill|lt=rainy season|Q658486|WD=Q658486|lt=samidare}} floods to the war-torn ruin of many provinces. === Sources === Source is 'Kosasato no Ayumi Ayama-chō' (Ayama Town Board of Education, ed.) (1980). The "Overview" section draws on pp.151-154; the "Anecdotes" section on pp.326 and 334. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related shrines === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabu ni Imasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyōgo)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabuta Myōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|WD=Q11409326}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yafuta Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Kawakuri District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊賀市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀市の歴史]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q24870457}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Yabuta Shrine |画像= [[File:陽夫多神社01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}951 Baba |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= Unknown
(currently {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; originally possibly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taga no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (Takamatsu deity) and Yabuta deity) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}})? |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} ({{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}) |別名= Yabuta Shrine, Takamatsu Gion, Kawai Tennō, Yabuta Gion-sha, Takamatsu Gion-sha |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715}} - {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}} |神事= Kakko Odori ({{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}})
Negai-no-Yama Odori ({{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}})
Rarashi (Rara Festival) ({{ill|lt=February 18|Q2343|WD=Q2343}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Uga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yabuta Shrine''' (やぶたじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=inner-listed shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=listed shrine}} (small shrine) in Baba, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Yabuta Shrine is an ancient shrine counted both as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and a {{ill|lt=Kokushi genzaisha|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|lt=Kokushi-genzai shrine}}, but its divine lineage is unclear. According to 'Tō Yabuta Jinja-ki,' while noting that the 'Engi Fudoki' is likely spurious, it records: "There was a deity on Kawaiyama in {{ill|lt=Abe District|Q11657437|WD=Q11657437}}, called Yabuta Myōjin; in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|lt=Takeohirokuni Oshitate no Sumeramikoto}}, year {{ill|lt=Tsuchinoe-Uma|Q905011|WD=Q905011}}, the {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673}} Taga no Muraji worshiped it; the deity worshiped was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}." Taga no Muraji were a lineage in {{ill|lt=Taga|Q1346747|WD=Q1346747}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, tracing ancestry to {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858}}; thus worship of Susanoo, child of Izanagi, is plausible. In '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}},' Yabuta deity is treated as the Shikinaisha, while Takamatsu deity is treated as a non-Shikinaisha. In other words, Yabuta deity and Takamatsu deity are considered different. Takamatsu deity already held the rank of Senior Sixth, then in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 3 (861) was promoted to Junior Fifth Lower together with Takakura deity, Awa deity, and Unane deity. In Jōgan 15, Awa deity and Unane deity were promoted again and entered the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, but Takakura deity and Yabuta deity did not advance and remained Kokushi genzaisha outside the Engishiki list. One interpretation is that the clan venerating Yabuta deity lost power in inter-clan political struggles. Takamatsu deity's lineage is also unclear, but {{ill|lt=Torigoe Kenzaburō|Q28354686|WD=Q28354686}} inferred a {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi-line}} connection, based on frequent use of the character "高" in Katsuragi-related divine names. From these studies, identifying Takamatsu deity and Yabuta deity as the same remains questionable. In a written petition by priests of Yabuta Shrine is the statement: "In ancient times, from Kawai village (present site), about five chō east, from a place called Fujiyama, the deity was transferred to this shrine." Since Fujiyama referred to the alluvial zone of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomoda River|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawai River|1=UNKNOWN}}, Yabuta deity was likely an agricultural deity worshiped there. === Cultural properties === === Mie Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * Gion Festival Negai-no-Yama rite === Iga City designated cultural properties === * Bonshō (temple bell) * Otabisho Kofun === Festivals === Rites such as the Kinen festival and Gion festival are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/85 Special Shinto Rites in Mie Prefecture]. === Anecdotes === * In the precincts is a dry {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} called "Baba no Miyai." It is said water springs from it only on Yabuta Shrine's festival day. This parallels the "Wakasa-i" rite at {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigatsu-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} and is one legend born from sacred-water belief tied to agricultural pre-harvest rites. * At the {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} on the south side stands a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} called {{ill|lt=Otabisho Kofun (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Otabisho Kofun}}, with its stone chamber exposed. The buried person is unknown and the tomb was looted. * Multiple kofun have also been confirmed on Miyayama behind the shrine, the grove revered as {{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}}. Together with Otabisho Kofun, they form the {{ill|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group}}. In the mountain are also medieval and Sengoku-period castle remains, and a walking route is maintained as "Yabuta Kofun no Mori." * In {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544}} 5 (1473), {{ill|lt=Kampaku|Q1199966|WD=Q1199966}} and {{ill|lt=Daijō-daijin|Q1157586|WD=Q1157586}} {{ill|lt=Ichijō Kaneyoshi|Q1656375|WD=Q1656375}} visited Yabuta Shrine and composed this poem while crossing Takamatsu Bridge (once in the precincts). {{Quotation|Unable to cross, swept by this floating world's waves; stepping on Kawai's bridge is perilous.|Ichijō Kaneyoshi|'Fuchigawa no Ki'}} :It is said to liken the dangerous bridge during the {{ill|lt=rainy season|Q658486|WD=Q658486|lt=samidare}} floods to the war-torn ruin of many provinces. === Sources === Source is 'Kosasato no Ayumi Ayama-chō' (Ayama Town Board of Education, ed.) (1980). The "Overview" section draws on pp.151-154; the "Anecdotes" section on pp.326 and 334. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related shrines === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabu ni Imasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyōgo)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabuta Myōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|WD=Q11409326}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yafuta Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Kawakuri District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊賀市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀市の歴史]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q24870457}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Yabuta Shrine |画像= [[File:陽夫多神社01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}951 Baba |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= Unknown
(currently {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}; originally possibly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taga no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (Takamatsu deity) and Yabuta deity) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894}})? |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} ({{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}) |別名= Yabuta Shrine, Takamatsu Gion, Kawai Tennō, Yabuta Gion-sha, Takamatsu Gion-sha |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715}} - {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}} |神事= Kakko Odori ({{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}})
Negai-no-Yama Odori ({{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788}})
Rarashi (Rara Festival) ({{ill|lt=February 18|Q2343|WD=Q2343}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Uga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yabuta Shrine''' (やぶたじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=inner-listed shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=listed shrine}} (small shrine) in Baba, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. === Overview === Yabuta Shrine is an ancient shrine counted both as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and a {{ill|lt=Kokushi genzaisha|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|lt=Kokushi-genzai shrine}}, but its divine lineage is unclear. According to 'Tō Yabuta Jinja-ki,' while noting that the 'Engi Fudoki' is likely spurious, it records: "There was a deity on Kawaiyama in {{ill|lt=Abe District|Q11657437|WD=Q11657437}}, called Yabuta Myōjin; in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|lt=Takeohirokuni Oshitate no Sumeramikoto}}, year {{ill|lt=Tsuchinoe-Uma|Q905011|WD=Q905011}}, the {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673}} Taga no Muraji worshiped it; the deity worshiped was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}." Taga no Muraji were a lineage in {{ill|lt=Taga|Q1346747|WD=Q1346747}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, tracing ancestry to {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858}}; thus worship of Susanoo, child of Izanagi, is plausible. In '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708}},' Yabuta deity is treated as the Shikinaisha, while Takamatsu deity is treated as a non-Shikinaisha. In other words, Yabuta deity and Takamatsu deity are considered different. Takamatsu deity already held the rank of Senior Sixth, then in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635}} 3 (861) was promoted to Junior Fifth Lower together with Takakura deity, Awa deity, and Unane deity. In Jōgan 15, Awa deity and Unane deity were promoted again and entered the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, but Takakura deity and Yabuta deity did not advance and remained Kokushi genzaisha outside the Engishiki list. One interpretation is that the clan venerating Yabuta deity lost power in inter-clan political struggles. Takamatsu deity's lineage is also unclear, but {{ill|lt=Torigoe Kenzaburō|Q28354686|WD=Q28354686}} inferred a {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi-line}} connection, based on frequent use of the character "高" in Katsuragi-related divine names. From these studies, identifying Takamatsu deity and Yabuta deity as the same remains questionable. In a written petition by priests of Yabuta Shrine is the statement: "In ancient times, from Kawai village (present site), about five chō east, from a place called Fujiyama, the deity was transferred to this shrine." Since Fujiyama referred to the alluvial zone of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomoda River|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawai River|1=UNKNOWN}}, Yabuta deity was likely an agricultural deity worshiped there. === Cultural properties === === Mie Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * Gion Festival Negai-no-Yama rite === Iga City designated cultural properties === * Bonshō (temple bell) * Otabisho Kofun === Festivals === Rites such as the Kinen festival and Gion festival are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/85 Special Shinto Rites in Mie Prefecture]. === Anecdotes === * In the precincts is a dry {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} called "Baba no Miyai." It is said water springs from it only on Yabuta Shrine's festival day. This parallels the "Wakasa-i" rite at {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigatsu-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} and is one legend born from sacred-water belief tied to agricultural pre-harvest rites. * At the {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} on the south side stands a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}} called {{ill|lt=Otabisho Kofun (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Otabisho Kofun}}, with its stone chamber exposed. The buried person is unknown and the tomb was looted. * Multiple kofun have also been confirmed on Miyayama behind the shrine, the grove revered as {{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158}}. Together with Otabisho Kofun, they form the {{ill|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group}}. In the mountain are also medieval and Sengoku-period castle remains, and a walking route is maintained as "Yabuta Kofun no Mori." * In {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544}} 5 (1473), {{ill|lt=Kampaku|Q1199966|WD=Q1199966}} and {{ill|lt=Daijō-daijin|Q1157586|WD=Q1157586}} {{ill|lt=Ichijō Kaneyoshi|Q1656375|WD=Q1656375}} visited Yabuta Shrine and composed this poem while crossing Takamatsu Bridge (once in the precincts). {{Quotation|Unable to cross, swept by this floating world's waves; stepping on Kawai's bridge is perilous.|Ichijō Kaneyoshi|'Fuchigawa no Ki'}} :It is said to liken the dangerous bridge during the {{ill|lt=rainy season|Q658486|WD=Q658486|lt=samidare}} floods to the war-torn ruin of many provinces. === Sources === Source is 'Kosasato no Ayumi Ayama-chō' (Ayama Town Board of Education, ed.) (1980). The "Overview" section draws on pp.151-154; the "Anecdotes" section on pp.326 and 334. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related shrines === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabu ni Imasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyōgo)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabuta Myōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|WD=Q11409326}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yafuta Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}{{ill|lt=Kawakuri District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊賀市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀市の歴史]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * やぶたじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7190005006026 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県伊賀市馬場951番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:陽夫多神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24870457}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Yabuta Shrine |画像= [[File:陽夫多神社01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820|1=Iga, Mie}}951 Baba |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= Unknown
(currently {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}; originally possibly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taga no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} (Takamatsu deity) and Yabuta deity) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} 3 ({{ill|lt=538|Q32894|WD=Q32894|1=538}})? |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} ({{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}) |別名= Yabuta Shrine, Takamatsu Gion, Kawai Tennō, Yabuta Gion-sha, Takamatsu Gion-sha |札所等= |例祭= {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715|1=July 31}} - {{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788|1=August 1}} |神事= Kakko Odori ({{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}})
Negai-no-Yama Odori ({{ill|lt=August 1|Q2788|WD=Q2788|1=August 1}})
Rarashi (Rara Festival) ({{ill|lt=February 18|Q2343|WD=Q2343|1=February 18}}) |地図= {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Uga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yabuta Shrine''' (やぶたじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=inner-listed shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=listed shrine}} (small shrine) in Baba, {{ill|lt=Iga|Q460820|WD=Q460820|1=Iga, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. === Overview === Yabuta Shrine is an ancient shrine counted both as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and a {{ill|lt=Kokushi genzaisha|Q118304363|WD=Q118304363|lt=Kokushi-genzai shrine|1=Kokushi genzaisha}}, but its divine lineage is unclear. According to 'Tō Yabuta Jinja-ki,' while noting that the 'Engi Fudoki' is likely spurious, it records: "There was a deity on Kawaiyama in {{ill|lt=Abe District|Q11657437|WD=Q11657437|1=Ahai district}}, called Yabuta Myōjin; in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|lt=Takeohirokuni Oshitate no Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Senka}}, year {{ill|lt=Tsuchinoe-Uma|Q905011|WD=Q905011|1=Yang Earth Horse}}, the {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} Taga no Muraji worshiped it; the deity worshiped was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}." Taga no Muraji were a lineage in {{ill|lt=Taga|Q1346747|WD=Q1346747|1=Taga, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, tracing ancestry to {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}; thus worship of Susanoo, child of Izanagi, is plausible. In '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}},' Yabuta deity is treated as the Shikinaisha, while Takamatsu deity is treated as a non-Shikinaisha. In other words, Yabuta deity and Takamatsu deity are considered different. Takamatsu deity already held the rank of Senior Sixth, then in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|1=Jōgan}} 3 (861) was promoted to Junior Fifth Lower together with Takakura deity, Awa deity, and Unane deity. In Jōgan 15, Awa deity and Unane deity were promoted again and entered the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, but Takakura deity and Yabuta deity did not advance and remained Kokushi genzaisha outside the Engishiki list. One interpretation is that the clan venerating Yabuta deity lost power in inter-clan political struggles. Takamatsu deity's lineage is also unclear, but {{ill|lt=Torigoe Kenzaburō|Q28354686|WD=Q28354686|1=Kenzaburō Torigoe}} inferred a {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908|lt=Katsuragi-line|1=Katsuragi clan}} connection, based on frequent use of the character "高" in Katsuragi-related divine names. From these studies, identifying Takamatsu deity and Yabuta deity as the same remains questionable. In a written petition by priests of Yabuta Shrine is the statement: "In ancient times, from Kawai village (present site), about five chō east, from a place called Fujiyama, the deity was transferred to this shrine." Since Fujiyama referred to the alluvial zone of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomoda River|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawai River|1=UNKNOWN}}, Yabuta deity was likely an agricultural deity worshiped there. === Cultural properties === === Mie Prefecture designated intangible folk cultural property === * Gion Festival Negai-no-Yama rite === Iga City designated cultural properties === * Bonshō (temple bell) * Otabisho Kofun === Festivals === Rites such as the Kinen festival and Gion festival are listed in [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1257927/1/85 Special Shinto Rites in Mie Prefecture]. === Anecdotes === * In the precincts is a dry {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} called "Baba no Miyai." It is said water springs from it only on Yabuta Shrine's festival day. This parallels the "Wakasa-i" rite at {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigatsu-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} and is one legend born from sacred-water belief tied to agricultural pre-harvest rites. * At the {{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} on the south side stands a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} called {{ill|lt=Otabisho Kofun (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Otabisho Kofun}}, with its stone chamber exposed. The buried person is unknown and the tomb was looted. * Multiple kofun have also been confirmed on Miyayama behind the shrine, the grove revered as {{ill|lt=chinju no mori|Q11651158|WD=Q11651158|1=Chinju no Mori}}. Together with Otabisho Kofun, they form the {{ill|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group (Iga)|UNKNOWN|lt=Miyayama Kofun Group}}. In the mountain are also medieval and Sengoku-period castle remains, and a walking route is maintained as "Yabuta Kofun no Mori." * In {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|WD=Q1193544|1=Bunmei}} 5 (1473), {{ill|lt=Kampaku|Q1199966|WD=Q1199966|1=kampaku}} and {{ill|lt=Daijō-daijin|Q1157586|WD=Q1157586|1=Daijō-daijin}} {{ill|lt=Ichijō Kaneyoshi|Q1656375|WD=Q1656375|1=Ichijō Kaneyoshi}} visited Yabuta Shrine and composed this poem while crossing Takamatsu Bridge (once in the precincts). {{Quotation|Unable to cross, swept by this floating world's waves; stepping on Kawai's bridge is perilous.|Ichijō Kaneyoshi|'Fuchigawa no Ki'}} :It is said to liken the dangerous bridge during the {{ill|lt=rainy season|Q658486|WD=Q658486|lt=samidare|1=East Asian rainy season}} floods to the war-torn ruin of many provinces. === Sources === Source is 'Kosasato no Ayumi Ayama-chō' (Ayama Town Board of Education, ed.) (1980). The "Overview" section draws on pp.151-154; the "Anecdotes" section on pp.326 and 334. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related shrines === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabu ni Imasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Tajima Province|Q1053727|WD=Q1053727|1=Tajima Province}}{{ill|lt=Yabu District (Hyōgo)|Q11666859|WD=Q11666859|lt=Yabu District|1=Yabu district}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yabuta Myōjin|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}{{ill|lt=Atsumi District|Q11409326|WD=Q11409326|1=Atsumi district}}) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yafuta Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}{{ill|lt=Kawakuri District|Q11553861|WD=Q11553861|1=Kawawa district}}) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やふたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yabuta Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊賀市の建築物]] [[Category:伊賀市の歴史]] [[Category:伊賀国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
